purpose: Land completed Ultraplan 11 program to canonical main, roll out to local k3d, prove operator surface, and decide cloud-dev posture without rolling out write-mode ahead of a real T1 endpoint proof.

cp1_main_landing.tool: amof promote-main --push (for amof + amof-ui); plain push (for workspace/opensandbox audit-only commit)

repos_landed.method: fast-forward merge to main then plain push
repos_landed.reason_no_promote_main: promote-main refuses paths under ecosystems/*/audit by design; audits are docs-only pushes per existing pattern

image_build.build_context: workspace root (/home/hotshot/work/amof-platform) for controlplane; repos/amof-ui for dashboard
image_build.registry_alias_fix: k3d node resolves images via k3d-amof-registry:5000 (not host.k3d.internal:5000). Helm repository values overridden accordingly.

workspace_hostpath_sync.reason: PYTHONPATH resolves /workspace/repos/amof/scripts before /app/scripts, so the running code is the k3d-node-local workspace snapshot, not the image layer.
workspace_hostpath_sync.method: tar stream via kubectl exec into the controlplane deployment

profiles.detail: Runpod is not configured. Set RUNPOD_API_KEY before listing profiles.

gc_dry_run.detail: Runpod is not configured. Set RUNPOD_API_KEY (and optionally RUNPOD_API_BASE).

dashboard_page.note: The page renders the 'Checking platform session' spinner pre-auth; once the operator logs in, the full /runpod page (Health, Profiles, Pods, GC panels) becomes active.

configmap_runpod_lifecycle.interpretation: local profile allows pod lifecycle mutations (consistent with values.yaml default and values-local.yaml)

cp3_operator_surface_proof.lane_usability_verdict: heavy lane is still UNUSABLE, correctly, because RUNPOD_API_KEY and RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL are not wired inside the control plane pod. This matches the pre-T1-proof reality; operator surface is ready but a real /models endpoint has not been proven yet.

cp4_cloud_dev_rollout_decision.decision: NO live helm upgrade against cloud-dev in this tranche
rationale: T1 pod-based endpoint proof is not complete
Heavy lane is unusable locally; same would be true on cloud-dev
Cloud-dev kubeconfig context is not present in the workspace kubeconfig (current-context is k3d-amof only); no write path exists from this session anyway
Landing the config into main is sufficient: the next operator-triggered cloud-dev deploy will pick up the readonly posture automatically
effective_behavior_when_deployed: Cloud-dev control plane refuses POST/DELETE on /api/v1/runpod/pods (HTTP 403)
GC and TTL enforce with dry_run=false refuse; dry_run=true still works (readonly visibility)
Heavy lane stays unusable until operator manually creates the runpod-auth secret AND a real T1 endpoint proof exists
operator_followups_before_cloud_dev_writ: Run repos/amof/scripts/runpod_t1_drive.py and capture a real /models 200 with a known pod_id
Set RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL in .env with the proven URL
Create the cloud-dev runpod-auth secret: kubectl create secret generic runpod-auth --from-literal=api-key=... --from-literal=openai-base-url=...
Copy repos/amof/docs/contracts/examples/runpod-profiles/runner_code_heavy.yaml to the runtime .amof/runpod-profiles/ before flipping lifecycleManagement to write

cp5_closure.cloud_dev_readonly_rollout_deferred_reas: No live cloud-dev helm upgrade this tranche because T1 endpoint proof is missing and cloud-dev kubeconfig is not attached to this workspace; readonly posture is codified in main and will apply on the next cloud-dev deploy
cp5_closure.heavy_lane_usable_blocker: RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL unset and no pod-based /models endpoint proven (T1 still operator-gated)

next_slice_name: UP11-2 T1 live pod-based endpoint proof
next_slice_scope_hint: Run repos/amof/scripts/runpod_t1_drive.py --profile gpt-oss-120b with --stop-on-success (single pod, ~$2-4 budget, 45 min kill timer)
Capture the verdict in an audit receipt at ecosystems/amof-platform/audit/YYYY-MM-DD-runpod-t1-pod-endpoint-proof.json
After usable verdict, set RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL in local .env, restart local control plane, confirm /api/v1/runpod/heavy-lane/health reports status=usable against the local stack
Only then reconsider cloud-dev write-mode admission
mutations_performed: git: 3 promote-main pushes to main (amof, amof-ui, opensandbox)
docker: 2 image builds + pushes to k3d-amof registry (controlplane, dashboard)
helm: 2 upgrades on amof release in amof-system namespace (rev 27 then rev 28)
kubectl: 2 tar-syncs into /workspace on the controlplane pod hostPath; 1 rollout restart
